home.title: Zakat Calculator
home.quranVerse: وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكَاةَ وَأَقْرِضُوا اللَّهَ قَرْضًا حَسَنًا ۚ وَمَا تُقَدِّمُوا لِأَنفُسِكُم مِّنْ خَيْرٍ تَجِدُوهُ عِندَ اللَّهِ هُوَ خَيْرًا وَأَعْظَمَ أَجْرًا
home.quranTranslation: "And establish prayer and give zakat and loan Allah a goodly loan. And whatever good you put forward for yourselves—you will find it with Allah. It is better and greater in reward."
home.currencyPrompt: Choose the currency you'll use for calculations
home.zakatDescription: Zakat is one of the five pillars of Islam. It is a mandatory charitable contribution, calculated as 2.5% of your eligible wealth, to be given to those in need.

steps.step1: Select your preferred currency and settings
steps.step2: Add your assets (cash, gold, investments, etc.)
steps.step3: Get instant calculation of your Zakat
steps.step4: Generate detailed report for your records

home.disclaimer: For personalized advice, especially in complex situations, consulting a knowledgeable scholar or a trusted Islamic financial advisor is recommended.

about.title: About
about.overview: Traditional Zakat calculators often lack the sophistication needed for today's complex financial situations. Interactive Zakat Calculator bridges this gap by handling nuances like partial ownership, rental income, and investment distinctions—giving you confidence in your calculations without the hassle.
about.openSource: This calculator is an open source project, and contributions are welcome! Whether you're interested in improving calculation accuracy, enhancing the UI, or fixing bugs, your help is appreciated.

privacy.title: Privacy first design
privacy.description: No sign-up or account is required—your data stays on your device. All calculations occur locally in your browser without any back-end storage.

comprehensive.title: Comprehensive coverage
comprehensive.description: Quickly calculate Zakat on various asset types—cash, stocks (active vs. passive investments), precious metals, real estate, crypto, and more.

feedback.title: Real-time, interactive feedback
feedback.description: See immediate updates as you adjust figures (e.g., gold weight, stock quantities) using auto-fetched current market prices via API.

summaries.title: Visual Summaries
summaries.description: View an overall breakdown of Zakatable vs. exempt amounts, so you understand precisely how each asset contributes.

about.methodologyText1: Interactive Zakat Calculator follows established Islamic principles for Zakat calculation. Our methodology is based on scholarly consensus regarding zakatable assets and calculation methods.
about.methodologyText2: We've worked hard to incorporate sources and guidelines from prominent scholars like Joe Bradford to make sure it's accurate and reliable (Check out his {link}). However, any feedback will help—please let us know if you run into any issues.
about.methodologyLinkText: best selling Zakat Guide on Amazon
about.disclaimerText: While we make every effort to ensure accuracy, Interactive Zakat Calculator is a tool to assist in calculations and should not replace scholarly advice for complex situations. For personalized guidance, please consult with a qualified Islamic scholar or financial advisor.
about.analyticsDisclaimer: We use Google Analytics to understand how users interact with the calculator. No personal financial data is ever collected or transmitted. All Zakat calculations remain entirely local in your browser.
about.contactText: For bugs, feedback, or questions, please reach out to:

dashboard.restoring: Please wait while we restore your calculations

summary.belowNisab: No Zakat due (Below Nisab or no zakatable assets)
summary.nisabTooltip: Nisab is the minimum amount of wealth that must be owned before Zakat becomes obligatory. It is calculated based on the value of either gold (85g) or silver (612.36g), whichever is lower.
summary.offlineMode: Using offline nisab calculation. Live metal prices unavailable in Replit environment.
summary.offlineModeGeneric: Using local metal price data for nisab calculations. Live prices unavailable.
summary.currencyConversionIssueText: There may be an issue with the PKR nisab values. The values appear suspiciously low. Please try refreshing the page or temporarily switch to USD for more accurate calculations.
summary.reconnectAttempt: Tried {count} {count, plural, one {time} other {times}} to reconnect.
summary.debtReceivablesDesc: Money owed to you counts toward your zakatable wealth if you reasonably expect repayment. Include these amounts in your total assets.
summary.debtLiabilitiesDesc: Outstanding debts you owe reduce your total zakatable wealth. These are subtracted from your assets before calculating Zakat.

digitalWallets.name: Digital Wallets (PayPal, Venmo, Apple Pay, etc.)
digitalWallets.description: Balance in digital payment services and mobile wallets

foreignCurrency.name: Foreign Currency
foreignCurrency.description: Any foreign currency holdings (converted to your local currency)

retirement.title: Retirement Accounts
retirement.calculationErrorDesc: There was an error calculating your retirement breakdown. Please check your inputs.

rental.title: Rental Property
rental.description: Net rental income is zakatable after expenses
rental.tooltip: Calculate Zakat on net rental income (income minus expenses) if Hawl is met

primary.title: Primary Residence
primary.description: Personal residence is exempt from Zakat
primary.tooltip: Primary residence for personal use is not subject to Zakat

sale.title: Property for Sale
sale.description: Property intended for sale is zakatable
sale.tooltip: Full market value is zakatable if property is actively for sale and Hawl is met

vacant.title: Vacant Land
vacant.tooltip: Land value is zakatable if intended for sale and Hawl requirement is met

report.title: Generate Report
report.description: Download a detailed PDF report of your Zakat calculation
report.error: Unable to generate report. Please try again.

distribution.title: Zakat Distribution
distribution.allocateDescription: Allocate your Zakat across these eight categories of eligible recipients (asnaf). Hover over any category to learn more about it.

poor.name: The Poor (Fuqara)
poor.description: Those who have little or no income and cannot meet their basic needs.

needy.name: The Needy (Masakin)
needy.description: Those who have some means but still cannot meet all their essential needs.

administrators.name: Zakat Administrators
administrators.description: Those who collect and distribute Zakat.

reconciliation.name: For Reconciliation (Muallafat-ul-Quloob)
reconciliation.description: Those whose hearts are inclined toward Islam or whose goodwill is sought.

freeing_captives.name: Freeing Captives (Riqab)
freeing_captives.description: Historically for freeing slaves; now applied to freeing those in bondage or oppression.

indebted.name: Those in Debt (Gharimeen)
indebted.description: People burdened with debt who cannot repay it.

cause_of_allah.name: In the Cause of Allah (Fi Sabilillah)
cause_of_allah.description: Efforts and projects that serve the greater good of the community and uphold Islamic values.

travelers.name: Travelers (Ibn as-Sabil)
travelers.description: Travelers who are stranded or in need while away from home.

hawl.label: Hawl Status
hawl.tooltip: Hawl refers to the completion of one full Islamic lunar year (approximately 354 days) during which the wealth has been in your possession and above the Nisab threshold.
hawl.description: Has this wealth been in your possession for one full lunar year?

nisab.label: Nisab
nisab.tooltip: Nisab is the minimum amount of wealth that must be owned before Zakat becomes obligatory. It is calculated based on the value of either gold (85g) or silver (612.36g), whichever is lower.
